title: Uruguay vs Cape Verde World Cup 2026 Predictions and Best Bets
original_text:
Group H of the 2026 FIFA World Cup throws up a fascinating contrast on Sunday, 21 June, when Uruguay face Cape Verde at Hard Rock Stadium in Miami Gardens.
Uruguay are two-time world champions looking to make amends after a group-stage exit in Qatar. Cape Verde are writing their World Cup story for the very first time.
This article covers the match preview, team news, predicted lineups, and the best bets for Uruguay vs Cape Verde.

[bm_toplist_customtheme_v2 id="77456"]

Detail
Info

Date
Sunday, 21 June 2026

Kick-off
22:00 BST

Venue
Hard Rock Stadium, Miami Gardens, USA

Stage
Group H, Matchday 2

TV (UK)
BBC / iPlayer

Group H standings (after Matchday 1):

Saudi Arabia: P1, D1, 1 pt
Uruguay: P1, D1, 1 pt
Cape Verde: P1, D1, 1 pt
Spain: P1, D1, 1 pt

What's at Stake

Every team in Group H starts Matchday 2 level on one point after a round of draws, which makes this fixture critical for both sides. Uruguay drew 1-1 with Saudi Arabia in their opener and need a victory to establish themselves as genuine contenders for one of the top two qualification spots. Cape Verde held Spain to a 0-0 draw, a result that announced their arrival on the World Cup stage, but a second draw here would leave them dangerously reliant on their final group game against Spain.

Verdict

Uruguay to win this match looks the most straightforward read in a tight group, with the quality of Federico Valverde, Manuel Ugarte, and Darwin Nunez giving them clear advantages across every area of the pitch. A Uruguay win at 1/2 represents fair value for a two-time world champion side against World Cup debutants who have yet to score at this tournament.

Uruguay vs Cape Verde Match Preview

Uruguay arrive in Miami carrying the weight of history and an urgent need for three points. Their 1-1 draw against Saudi Arabia, with Maximiliano Araujo finding the net, was not the statement result their group campaign required. Marcelo Bielsa has built this squad around a core of European-club talent, and this is the match where that quality should tell.

Cape Verde have earned enormous credit simply by reaching this stage. Their goalless draw against Spain was a disciplined, organised performance that demonstrated exactly how manager Rui Aguas has set this team up: compact, hard to break down, and dangerous on the counter. Veterans like Ryan Mendes and Garry Rodrigues provide experience, while Dailon Livramento has been their sharpest attacking threat in recent matches.

The key question is whether Cape Verde's defensive structure, which frustrated Spain, can absorb the pressure that a Bielsa-coached Uruguay will generate across 90 minutes. Uruguay have the personnel to play through a low block. Cape Verde will need to be at their disciplined best to take anything from the game.

Team Form

Uruguay - last five matches:

Saudi Arabia (N): Drew 1-1 - FIFA World Cup
Algeria (N): Drew 0-0 - Friendly
England (A): Drew 1-1 - Friendly
United States (A): Lost 1-5 - Friendly
Mexico (A): Drew 0-0 - Friendly

Uruguay have drawn four of their last five matches and have not won since their pre-tournament friendlies. The 5-1 loss to the United States raised eyebrows, though it came in a non-competitive context. Their World Cup opener against Saudi Arabia suggests they are capable of scoring, but the inability to win matches in the build-up will concern Bielsa ahead of a game they are expected to take.

Cape Verde - last five matches:

Spain (A): Drew 0-0 - FIFA World Cup
Bermuda (N): Lost 0-3 - Friendly
Serbia (N): Won 3-0 - Friendly
Finland (N): Drew 1-1 - FIFA Series
Chile (N): Lost 2-4 - FIFA Series

Cape Verde's recent form is mixed, including a 3-0 loss to Bermuda that came just before the tournament. However, their competitive record is more encouraging, and holding Spain scoreless on Matchday 1 is a genuine statement. Rui Aguas' side tend to raise their game in competitive fixtures, and their African qualifying campaign of five wins from six matches underlines that quality.

Uruguay vs Cape Verde Head to Head

There is no prior meeting between Uruguay and Cape Verde at senior international level. This Group H fixture is their first-ever encounter, meaning there is no head-to-head record to draw on when assessing the Uruguay vs Cape Verde odds. Every piece of history between these two nations begins here in Miami.

Team News

Uruguay's squad is headlined by Real Madrid midfielder Federico Valverde, Barcelona defender Ronald Araujo, and Manchester United midfielder Manuel Ugarte. Marcelo Bielsa has overseen a generational shift in this group, moving on from the era of Luis Suarez and Edinson Cavani to build around a younger, European-tested core. Darwin Nunez of Al-Hilal leads the attacking line with 13 international goals to his name.

Maximiliano Araujo scored Uruguay's only goal of the tournament so far and has been among the sharpest performers in recent matches, making him one to watch again here. Goalkeeper Fernando Muslera, now 40, is the veteran presence in goal with 134 caps for his country. There are no reported injuries or suspensions within the squad that would affect selection for this fixture.

Cape Verde name Rui Aguas as their manager, and his squad leans on experienced players in key positions. Goalkeeper Vozinha, aged 40, brings 86 caps of international experience between the posts. Ryan Mendes, with 98 caps and 22 international goals, is their most dangerous attacking threat alongside Dailon Livramento, who has been prolific in recent matches. No significant absences have been reported ahead of this fixture.

Predicted Lineups

Uruguay (4-3-3): Muslera; Varela, Gimenez, R. Araujo, Olivera; Ugarte, Valverde, Bentancur; Pellistri, Nunez, M. Araujo

Cape Verde (4-4-2): Vozinha; Stopira, Roberto Lopes, Logan Costa, Steven Moreira; Garry Rodrigues, Jamiro Monteiro, Deroy Duarte, Jovane Cabral; Ryan Mendes, Dailon Livramento

Predicted lineups based on available squad information. Squads to be confirmed before kick-off.

Key Tactical Matchup

The contest between Manuel Ugarte and Cape Verde's midfield pair of Jamiro Monteiro and Deroy Duarte will go a long way to deciding this match. Ugarte's role as a ball-winner and distributor from deep is central to how Bielsa wants Uruguay to control possession and set the tempo. With 36 caps and extensive experience at Manchester United, he has the profile to dominate a midfield battle. Cape Verde's two central midfielders combined for 85 caps in qualifying and are disciplined rather than creative, meaning if Ugarte can bypass them quickly, he unlocks the space that Valverde and Bentancur need to drive Uruguay forward.

Best Bets

[bm_toplist_customtheme_v2 id="77453"]

Uruguay to win @ 1/2
This is the foundation bet for this fixture. Uruguay carry the individual quality of Valverde, Ugarte, Nunez, and Araujo into a match against World Cup debutants who have yet to score in the tournament. The 1/2 price reflects their clear superiority on paper, and while Cape Verde showed resilience against Spain, Uruguay should have enough to find a winning goal. A point feels like an unsatisfactory return for a side of their pedigree against this opposition.

Under 2.5 goals @ 4/6
Cape Verde held Spain to a 0-0 draw and conceded zero goals in their World Cup opener. Uruguay themselves have drawn four of their last five matches, with goals hard to come by in several of those fixtures. The totals market prices under 2.5 goals at 4/6, which looks reasonable given the defensive discipline Cape Verde have shown and Uruguay's tendency to grind results rather than produce high-scoring games. A 1-0 or 2-0 scoreline seems the most likely outcome.

Darwin Nunez anytime scorer
Nunez has scored 13 international goals from 38 caps for Uruguay and is the focal point of Bielsa's attacking system. Against a Cape Verde defence that has not yet been tested by a forward of his physical profile and pace, he represents one of the stronger anytime scorer options in this fixture. Check your preferred operator for the best available price on Nunez to score at any point during the match.

Uruguay to win and under 2.5 goals (bet builder)
Combining Uruguay to win with under 2.5 goals is a natural fit for this fixture given the evidence. This Uruguay vs Cape Verde bet builder combination captures the most likely scenario: a narrow Uruguayan victory, possibly 1-0 or 2-0, against an organised Cape Verde side that rarely opens up and has not scored in the tournament yet. Combining these two legs should enhance the return on what is otherwise a short-priced favourite win.

How to Watch and How to Bet

How to Watch

Uruguay vs Cape Verde is available to watch live and free in the UK on BBC and via BBC iPlayer. Kick-off is at 22:00 BST on Sunday, 21 June 2026, with the match taking place at Hard Rock Stadium in Miami Gardens, USA.

How to Bet

If you are looking to place a bet on this fixture, follow these steps to do so responsibly and efficiently.

Check the match odds across multiple operators to ensure you are getting the best available price.
Review the team news and predicted lineups before finalising any selections.
Decide on your stake before you log in and stick to it.
Consider whether a single, accumulator, or bet builder best suits your selections.
Look at the goals markets as well as the match result for additional options.
Use any available promotions such as enhanced odds or free bets, but read the terms carefully.
Keep records of your bets so you can review your decisions over time.
Only bet what you can afford to lose, and never chase losses from previous wagers.

Responsible Gambling

Betting should always be approached as a form of entertainment, not a reliable source of income. Set a budget before you start and treat any losses as the cost of that entertainment rather than a problem to be recovered.

If you feel that your gambling is becoming difficult to control, free and confidential support is available. In the UK, you can contact GamCare on 0808 8020 133 or visit gamcare.org.uk . Gamblers Anonymous UK can also be reached at gamblersanonymous.org.uk .

Most betting operators offer tools such as deposit limits, cooling-off periods, and self-exclusion. Use them if you need to take a step back.

Closing Thoughts

Uruguay vs Cape Verde is one of the more straightforward-looking fixtures of the World Cup 2026 group stage on paper, though Cape Verde's performance against Spain showed they are not here simply to make up the numbers.

A narrow Uruguay victory, with the goals market staying under 2.5, feels like the most grounded prediction given what both sides have shown so far. The quality of Valverde, Ugarte, and Nunez should be decisive, but Cape Verde's organisation means this is unlikely to be a routine afternoon.

Whatever your selections, ensure they are based on your own assessment of the evidence and placed within a budget you are comfortable with.
